steps.title: The Blocker
steps.text: Interference tactics work when an enemy piece is defending a key square along a line — you interpose your own piece to block that defense. Often decisive in the middlegame.

steps.title: Novotny and Plachutta
steps.text: Two classical interference patterns: the Novotny (sacrificing on a crossing of two lines, forcing one defender to block the other) and the Plachutta (same idea but with like-moving pieces). Beautiful geometrical tactics.
